title: Define L2 payload discriminator for raw routing announces vs SCHC
description: Why this exists: production L2 raw announce dispatch is unsafe today because raw announce Type=0x01 collides with SCHC_RULE_GLOBAL_COAP=1 and canonical global CoAP compressed bytes start with 0x01. Link specs currently describe L2 payload as SCHC-compressed IPv6 while routing specs describe raw announce messages without a link payload namespace. What needs to be done: define an explicit authenticated inner-payload discriminator/wrapper (for example SCHC dispatch 0x14 plus a separate raw routing/announce dispatch namespace), update specs/constants/vectors across C/Python/Rust as needed, and add no-hardware tests proving global CoAP SCHC and raw announces cannot be confused.
notes:
Implemented authenticated L2 inner payload discriminator for SCHC vs routing/control.

Scope completed:
- Defined dispatch 0x14 = SCHC body and 0x15 = routing/control body in constants.toml, Python constants/helpers, Rust lichen-core, Zephyr l2_payload.h, specs, and vectors.
- Updated Python Node TX/RX: SCHC sends use 0x14||SCHC, announces/scheduled routing use 0x15||body, NodeChannel only decompresses 0x14 SCHC, KISS REJ moved to private 0x7f 0x15 prefix so all 0x15 payloads remain L2 routing/control.
- Updated Rust lichen-node stack/node and lichen-gateway to require/wrap 0x14||SCHC at mesh boundaries.
- Updated Zephyr lichen_link_tx to sign/MIC 0x14||SCHC and lichen_link_rx to require 0x14 before SCHC decompression.
- Added C gateway announce L2 wrapper entry point gateway_announce_ingest_l2_payload() requiring 0x15 routing dispatch; production routing-owner wiring remains covered by project-LICHEN-8ko4/project-LICHEN-ct4d.
- Added canonical l2_payload.json vectors and schema/generator coverage.
